---
title: "Graphical Perception Experiment"
subtitle: "Classroom Activity - Cleveland & McGill Replication"
author: ""
format:
  pdf:
    geometry: margin=1in
---

### Trial 1: Bar Chart

Which is smaller? _______

What percentage is the smaller of the larger? _______%

### Trial 2: Bar Chart

Which is smaller? _______

What percentage is the smaller of the larger? _______%

### Trial 3: Pie Chart

Which is smaller? _______

What percentage is the smaller of the larger? _______%

### Trial 4: Pie Chart

Which is smaller? _______

What percentage is the smaller of the larger? _______%

### Trial 5: Bubble Chart

Which is smaller? _______

What percentage is the smaller of the larger? _______%

### Trial 6: Bubble Chart

Which is smaller? _______

What percentage is the smaller of the larger? _______%

\newpage

### Your Error Calculations

**Error = |Your estimate - True percent|**

| Trial | Your Estimate | True % | Error |
|-------|--------------|--------|-------|
| 1 (Bar) | _____% | ____% | _____ |
| 2 (Bar) | _____% | ____% | _____ |
| 3 (Pie) | _____% | ____% | _____ |
| 4 (Pie) | _____% | ____% | _____ |
| 5 (Bubble) | _____% | ____% | _____ |
| 6 (Bubble) | _____% | ____% | _____ |

### Average Error by Chart Type

**Bar Chart Average Error:** (Error 1 + Error 2) / 2 = _______

**Pie Chart Average Error:** (Error 3 + Error 4) / 2 = _______

**Bubble Chart Average Error:** (Error 5 + Error 6) / 2 = _______
